_note: PR 4 (006 T118) — AEA exemption commingling (canned-string dominates calendar date). CAPCO-2016 §E.4 pp33-34: 'When a derivatively classified NSI document contains portions of Restricted Data (RD), Formerly Restricted Data (FRD), or Transclassified Foreign Nuclear Information (TFNI), the "Declassify On" line must not contain a declassification date or event. The following must be annotated on the "Declassify On" line: "N/A to [RD/FRD/TFNI, as appropriate] portions" and "See source list for NSI portions" separated by a period.' Fixture exercises the CAB-commingling structural shape (Classified-By / Derived-From / Declassify-On block); CapcoScheme's `DeclassExemptionAccumulator` last-observed semantics + the `DeclassifyOnLattice` MaxDate would compose this; the engine's `lint` pass over the raw bytes emits zero diagnostics today (the AEA canned-string passes the scanner's CAB heuristic and the rule chain does not enforce the §E.4 NSI-vs-AEA preference at the surface level yet).
